data.narrative:
Greenroom Robotics is a Series A–stage defense and security robotics company founded by Harry Hubbert. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of two public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private companies operating in the same sector, with primary reliance on three same-stage, same-sector private peers: Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

The peer-median method applied to these three comparable companies yields a valuation of $300M. The peer set spans a range from $150M (Ares Industries) to $500M (Stark Defence), reflecting material dispersion in how the market values Series A defense-tech operators. This median-based approach is the primary quantitative anchor for the analysis, given the availability of directly comparable staging and sectoral positioning.

The triangulated valuation range is $255M to $345M, with a median of $300M. This narrow band reflects limited methodological triangulation—only the peer-median approach yielded reliable inputs from available data. The $150M–$500M span of actual peer valuations underscores that stage-appropriate comps in this sector exhibit 3.3x valuation dispersion, a material consideration when assessing Greenroom's positioning within this range. Key uncertainties include Greenroom's relative technical maturity, customer traction, and capital efficiency versus named peers, none of which are quantified in the available dataset.
